name: Syrups
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat equal parts water and sugar until combined.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil.
3. Pour over raw fruits in jars for chilled or raw packs, or fill into heated jars after boiling water and sugar have been brought back to a boil with fruit added.
4. For hot packs, add fruit to boiling syrup and reheat to a boil before filling jars.
Other sweeteners: Light corn syrups or mild-flavored honey may replace up to half the table sugar in syrups for more information see Canned Foods for Special Diets.
Table 1. Preparing and using syrups.
(Measure of Water and Sugar)
Syrup Type: Very Light. Approx. % Sugar: 10
For 9-Pint Load: 6-1/2 Cups Water, 3/4 Cup Sugar
For 7-Quart Load: 10-1/2 Cups Water, 1-1/4 Cups Sugar
Syrup Type: Light. Approx. % Sugar: 20
For 9-Pint Load: 5-3/4 Cups Water, 1-1/2 Cups Sugar
For 7-Quart Load: 9 Cups Water, 2-1/4 Cups Sugar
Syrup Type: Medium. Approx. % Sugar: 30
For 9-Pint Load: 5-1/4 Cups Water, 2-1/4 Cups Sugar
For 7-Quart Load: 8-1/4 Cups Water, 3-3/4 Cups Sugar
Syrup Type: Heavy. Approx % Sugar: 40
For 9-Pint Load: 5 Cups Water, 3-1/4 Cups Sugar
For 7-Quart Load: 7-3/4 Cups Water, 5-1/4 Cups Sugar
Syrup Type: Very Heavy. Approx % Sugar: 50
For 9-Pint Load: 4-1/4 Cups Water, 4-1/4 Cups Sugar
For 7-Quart Load: 6-1/2 Cups Water, 6-3/4 Cups Sugar
